description: Connect to OneDrive (personal or business) to browse and pull files into your knowledge base. Works with any Microsoft account.

operations.name: Health Check
operations.description: Verify the connection — checks the access token and that OneDrive is reachable.

operations.name: List Files & Folders
operations.description: List files and folders in your OneDrive (root or a subfolder)

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Folder ID
parameters.description: Folder ID to browse into (leave empty for root)

operations.name: Get File Info
operations.description: Get metadata for a specific file or folder in OneDrive

operations.name: Download File
operations.description: Download a file from OneDrive to the server's uploads folder

operations.name: Search Files
operations.description: Search OneDrive for files by name or content

operations.name: Import to Knowledge Base
operations.description: Download a OneDrive file and add it to an agent's knowledge base for AI-powered retrieval

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.label: Agent ID
parameters.description: The agent to add this document to as knowledge

parameters.name: description
parameters.label: Description
parameters.description: Optional description for this knowledge item

setup_instructions:
## OneDrive Setup

Works with **personal Microsoft accounts** (outlook.com, hotmail.com) or **work/school accounts**.

### Option A: Reuse Existing SharePoint Azure App
If you already configured a SharePoint Online integration, you can reuse the same Azure app:
1. Azure Portal → App Registrations → your existing app
2. **Authentication** → change **Supported account types** to **Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts**
3. **API Permissions** → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Delegated → add `Files.Read`
4. Copy your existing client ID and secret into Local Secrets:
   - `OAUTH_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID`
   - `OAUTH_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET`

### Option B: New Azure App
1. Azure Portal → [App Registrations](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade) → **New registration**
2. Name: `AI Hub OneDrive Integration`
3. **Supported account types**: choose **Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts** (works for both work and personal)
4. **Redirect URI**: Web → `{callback_url}`
5. Click **Register**
6. **API Permissions** → Add → Microsoft Graph → Delegated → add `Files.Read` and `offline_access`. (No admin consent required for personal accounts.)
7. **Certificates & secrets** → New client secret → copy the **Value** immediately
8. Store in Local Secrets:
   - `OAUTH_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID` → Application (client) ID from the Overview page
   - `OAUTH_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET` → the secret value

### Connect
1. Click **Connect** in the Integrations Gallery
2. Sign in with your Microsoft account (personal or work)
3. Approve the requested permissions

Once connected, the OneDrive browser will show your files directly — no site/library navigation needed.
